title: abutilon carinatum
intro: Abutilon carinatum, also known as the hairy gourd or longleaf abutilon, is a flowering plant species in the mallow family Malvaceae. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, and its roots and leaves are rich in medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal compounds. The health benefits of Abutilon carinatum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to protect against cancer and other diseases. Abutilon carinatum can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its roots and leaves can also be used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
